name: Utility Scoring
description: An AI decision model that scores possible actions and selects one based on weighted context.
design_notes: Utility scoring works best when designers can inspect why an action won. Each score should represent a clear desire or pressure, and the system should include clamps, hysteresis, or commitment rules to avoid flicker.
design_purpose: Let AI choose between competing goals using context.
Support emergent behavior without hard-coding every branch.
Expose tunable decision weights for designers.
player_fantasy: The player feels that agents respond flexibly to changing world state.

parameters.name: candidate_action_count
parameters.description: Number of actions considered by a decision pass.

parameters.name: commitment_min_ms
parameters.description: Minimum window before changing away from the selected action.

parameters.name: score_hysteresis_margin
parameters.description: Margin required for a new action to replace the current action.

edge_cases: Equal scores need deterministic tie-breaking or intentional randomness.
Missing blackboard values should not collapse scores to misleading defaults.
Frequent rescoring can interrupt animations and navigation constantly.
common_bugs: Scores are not normalized and one consideration dominates every decision.
AI switches actions every tick because no commitment window exists.
Debug output shows final scores but not the considerations that caused them.
balancing_notes: Utility AI needs designer-facing visualization or tuning becomes guesswork.
Commitment and hysteresis are as important as raw scores for believable behavior.
accessibility_notes: When utility drives opponents, keep player-readable tells so behavior feels reactive rather than random.

implementation_notes.unity: Represent actions and considerations as data assets or plain objects and add debug views for per-consideration scores.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Resources for score curves and a blackboard dictionary, then select actions on a fixed decision cadence.
implementation_notes.unreal: Combine Utility AI plugins or custom score evaluators with Behavior Tree/StateTree tasks for execution.
implementation_notes.web: Compute utility scores in pure functions over AI state so decisions can be logged, replayed, and tested.
